cases.prompt: Please run the Eval System adviser pass on this sandbox. The `.zoto/eval-system/` tree here only has manifest metadata—I never installed `config.yml`—so walk `zoto-eval-adviser` through its first-load checks before any target scans.
assertions: Returned payload is structured `needs_user_input` whose guard text cites `/z-eval-configure` before any dimensional scoring.
The agent returned structured `needs_user_input` without emitting askQuestion from the subagent loop while surfacing that blocker.
No narrative cites newly opened SKILL.md, command Markdown, agent Markdown, or evals.json excerpts because discovery config never loaded.

files.content:
schema_version: 1
updated_at: 2026-05-01T12:00:00Z
last_scan:
  status: staged
targets: []
discovery_config: {}
eval_roots: []

cases.expected_output: Structured needs_user_input tells me to unblock discovery with `/z-eval-configure`; no adviser_report payloads yet.

cases.prompt: Delegate `zoto-eval-adviser` inside a sandbox where `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml` exists but the paired manifest YAML is absent—we never ran discovery scaffolding—and paste what comes back before any coverage narration.
assertions: `needs_user_input` cites `/z-eval-create` immediately after loaders detect the missing manifest.
The agent returned structured guidance without emitting askQuestion from the subagent loop.
No per-target SKILL.md excerpts or evals.json quotes appear because the manifest never enumerated rows for this sandbox.

files.content:
evalsDir: evals
skillsRoots:
  - plugins/*/skills
discoveryTargets:
  - skill
ignore: []

cases.expected_output: Structured needs_user_input points to `/z-eval-create`; dimensional scoring waits until manifests exist.

cases.prompt: I need you to analyse the full Eval System manifest under the full-scan scope that `/z-eval-advise` already forwarded through this Task. Have `zoto-advise-evals` score trigger phrases, schema validation coverage, regression grader breadth, citation checks, and spec checklist completeness for each listed primitive, then halt at drill-down breakpoint one so sidebar answers can resume the flow.
assertions: Breakpoint one returns structured `needs_user_input` for drill-down dimensions without emitting askQuestion from the subagent loop.
The adviser_report sections align with trigger-phrase, schema-validation, regression-baseline, context-citation, and status-checklist completeness dimensions from Advise Mode.
The prose explains that `zoto-advise-evals` produced the structured scoring before the pause.
Disk copies of `.zoto/eval-system/manifest.yml`, `.zoto/eval-system/config.yml`, and every referenced `evals/evals.json` remain byte-identical to their pre-run baselines.
The write-up never copies paths or excerpts from `evals/_runs`, `report.yml`, `static.yml`, or `llm.yml` trees that belong to completed evaluator runs.
cases.expected_output: The structured adviser_report lists per-target notes for all five gap dimensions with evidence drawn from source markdown and existing eval assertions, pausing with needs_user_input for drill-down choices.

cases.prompt: Resume after I picked Trigger-Phrase plus Schema in the drill-down UI: deepen those dimensions, map each gap to exactly one follow-on command—`/z-eval-create` for greenfield targets or `/z-eval-update --target plugins/**/skills --apply` when rows already exist—then stop at breakpoint two so I can accept or reject bundles before returning to `/z-eval-advise`.
follow_ups: I accepted every breakpoint-two bundle that proposed edits; close the pass with the consolidated adviser_report listing the approved `/z-eval-create` and `/z-eval-update` hand-offs for the executor.
assertions: Breakpoint two keeps guidance inside structured `needs_user_input`, still honoring the no askQuestion-from-adviser contract.

Each gap resolves to exactly one deterministic route—`/z-eval-create` when no eval file exists versus `/z-eval-update` with `--target` and `--apply` when hardened rows demand refresh—with no competing dual commands.
The closing adviser_report lists only the accepted command hand-offs and never rewrites manifest hashes or eval JSON directly.
cases.expected_output: Breakpoint two surfaces structured accept-or-reject needs_user_input, then the concluding turn merges the adviser_report once host answers finalize the selections.
